skill.name: rdkit
skill.description: RDKit guidance for cheminformatics workflows, including molecular parsing, descriptors, fingerprints, substructure search, similarity screening, reactions, and 2D or 3D molecule generation.
skill.summary: Use RDKit for advanced molecular analysis, screening, and cheminformatics automation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but targeted review found most were false positives from Markdown backticks, RDKit API names, and chemical SMARTS notation. No prompt injection, network access, subprocess execution, or credential exfiltration evidence was found. The confirmed risks are local filesystem writes in helper scripts and documentation that demonstrates pickle loading without an explicit trust warning.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed Local File Writes
medium_findings.description: The helper scripts write CSV, SDF, SMILES, or report files to paths supplied by command-line arguments. This is legitimate for cheminformatics workflows, but it can overwrite local files if users provide unsafe paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file write calls use output paths passed by the user. No malicious intent is shown, but the filesystem capability is real.

medium_findings.title: Pickle Loading Guidance Requires Trust Boundary Warning
medium_findings.description: The documentation recommends pickle for fast molecule storage and shows loading a pickle file. Pickle loading can execute code if the file is untrusted, so this guidance needs a clear trust-boundary warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly imports pickle and calls pickle.load. This is a known Python risk when files are not trusted.

medium_findings.title: Executable Helper Scripts Operate on Local Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill includes command-line Python scripts that parse local molecule files and produce analysis results. No network, subprocess, environment-secret access, or credential exfiltration was found, but script execution increases review requirements.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are executable Python scripts with argparse interfaces. Their behavior is domain-specific and local, not malicious.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts are caused by Markdown backticks around Python API names and code blocks. They document RDKit calls and are not Ruby backtick execution or shell command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and Python examples, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. No command execution path is present there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Chemical Patterns Flagged as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: C2 keyword, weak-crypto, and obfuscation alerts are caused by chemical terms, descriptor names, and SMARTS strings. The cited content is used for molecular substructure matching, not malware control, cryptography, or JavaScript obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a SMARTS and descriptor reference for RDKit. The suspicious tokens are chemical notation and descriptor labels.

low_findings.title: No Evidence of Network or Secret Exfiltration
low_findings.description: Targeted review found no requests, socket usage, subprocess execution, environment-secret reads, or outbound endpoints in the reviewed files. The scripts are limited to local cheminformatics processing and file output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Imports and reviewed code show argparse, pathlib, sys, csv, and RDKit use. No network or secret-handling code was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Directed Local File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts write CSV, SDF, SMILES, or report files to paths supplied by command-line arguments. This is legitimate for cheminformatics workflows, but it can overwrite local files if users provide unsafe paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct file write calls use output paths passed by the user. No malicious intent is shown, but the filesystem capability is real.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pickle Loading Guidance Requires Trust Boundary Warning
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation recommends pickle for fast molecule storage and shows loading a pickle file. Pickle loading can execute code if the file is untrusted, so this guidance needs a clear trust-boundary warning.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly imports pickle and calls pickle.load. This is a known Python risk when files are not trusted.

content.value_statement: Molecular analysis often needs precise parsing, descriptors, fingerprints, and substructure logic. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use RDKit APIs and helper scripts for reproducible cheminformatics work.
actual_capabilities: Explains RDKit molecule creation from SMILES, SDF, MOL, PDB, InChI, and sequence inputs.
Guides descriptor calculation for molecular weight, LogP, TPSA, Lipinski rules, QED, and ring features.
Provides SMARTS patterns for functional groups, rings, PAINS filters, charged groups, and common scaffolds.
Supports fingerprint and similarity workflows using Morgan, RDKit, MACCS, atom pair, and torsion fingerprints.
Includes helper scripts for molecular properties, similarity search, and substructure filtering.
Documents molecule drawing, coordinate generation, reactions, conformers, and batch-processing patterns.
limitations: Requires RDKit to be installed in the execution environment before examples or scripts can run.
Does not validate scientific conclusions; users must review chemistry assumptions and input quality.
Bundled scripts read and write local files using user-provided paths.
Pickle examples should only be used with trusted files because pickle loading can execute code.

use_cases.title: Screen Compound Libraries
use_cases.description: Run substructure and similarity workflows to identify candidate molecules from SDF or SMILES files.

use_cases.title: Prepare Molecular Features
use_cases.description: Calculate descriptors and fingerprints for model features, clustering, or exploratory data analysis.

use_cases.title: Build RDKit Pipelines
use_cases.description: Use API references and scripts to create reproducible molecular parsing, filtering, and reporting tools.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Basic Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the RDKit skill to analyze this SMILES string: [SMILES]. Explain molecular weight, LogP, TPSA, hydrogen bond counts, and Lipinski status.

prompt_templates.title: Find Functional Groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the RDKit SMARTS references to identify functional groups in these molecules: [MOLECULE LIST]. Include the SMARTS patterns used and explain each match.

prompt_templates.title: Design Similarity Screening
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an RDKit similarity screening plan for query [QUERY] against [DATABASE]. Compare fingerprint choices, thresholds, and output fields.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Cheminformatics Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this RDKit workflow for parsing, sanitization, descriptor calculation, and file handling risks: [WORKFLOW]. Suggest robust checks and safer defaults.

output_examples.input: Analyze ethanol from the SMILES string CCO.
output_examples.output: The skill would describe ethanol as a small polar molecule, report core descriptors, and explain why it passes simple drug-likeness thresholds.

output_examples.input: Filter a compound set for aromatic amines and remove PAINS alerts.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend SMARTS patterns, explain include and exclude filters, and outline a reproducible RDKit filtering workflow.

output_examples.input: Compare Morgan and MACCS fingerprints for similarity search.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare structural detail, interpretability, common thresholds, and when each fingerprint is suitable for screening.

best_practices: Validate every parsed molecule before descriptor, fingerprint, or substructure operations.
Use explicit output paths in a dedicated working directory when running helper scripts.
Treat pickle files and external molecule datasets as untrusted unless their source is verified.
anti_patterns: Do not assume all SMILES or SDF records parse successfully without checking for null molecules.
Do not load pickle files from unknown sources for molecule caching or performance tests.
Do not treat descriptor values as scientific conclusions without domain review and data validation.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants use RDKit for molecular parsing, descriptors, fingerprints, substructure search, visualization, and screening workflows.

faq.question: Does it install RDKit?
faq.answer: No. RDKit must already be installed before executable examples or helper scripts can run.

faq.question: Can it process SDF and SMILES files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation and scripts cover SDF, MOL, SMILES, and text-based molecule inputs.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The bundled Python scripts can read local molecule files and write outputs to user-provided paths.

faq.question: Is pickle usage safe?
faq.answer: Only with trusted files. Loading untrusted pickle data can execute code in Python.
